---
title: WRDS to PostgreSQL
---

Use this path when you want to materialize WRDS data into a PostgreSQL database
instead of writing Parquet files.

## Main helpers

- `wrds_update_pg(table_name, schema, ...)` creates or refreshes a PostgreSQL
  table from a WRDS source table.
- `wrds_pg_to_pg(table_name, schema, ...)` copies a WRDS PostgreSQL table into
  another PostgreSQL database without the update check.

## What it does

`wrds_update_pg()` plans a `SELECT` against WRDS, creates the destination table
from that query shape, copies rows into PostgreSQL, and stores WRDS table
metadata in the destination comment for update checks.

## Typical example

```python
from db2pq import wrds_update_pg

wrds_update_pg(
    table_name="dsi",
    schema="crsp",
    user="postgres",
    host="localhost",
    dbname="analytics",
)
```

## More examples

A WRDS-to-PostgreSQL load with explicit integer coercions for common CRSP
identifier columns:

```python
from db2pq import wrds_update_pg

wrds_update_pg(
    "dsf",
    "crsp",
    col_types={"permno": "integer", "permco": "integer"},
)
```

An Audit Analytics-style example where you compute a drop list first and then
materialize the cleaned table:

```python
from db2pq import wrds_update_pg

wrds_update_pg(
    table_name="feed55_auditor_ratification",
    schema="audit",
    drop=["match_score", "prior_match_flag", "closest_date"],
    col_types={
        "auditor_ratification_fkey": "integer",
        "share_class_fkey": "integer",
        "auditor_fkey": "integer",
        "pcaob_registration_number": "integer",
    },
)
```

After loading a table, it is natural to add indexes or build derived tables in
the destination PostgreSQL database:

```python
from db2pq import process_sql

process_sql("CREATE INDEX ON crsp.dsf (permno, date)")
process_sql("CREATE INDEX ON crsp.dsf (permco)")
```

For some workflows, you may also materialize a derived table after loading the
source WRDS tables:

```python
from db2pq import process_sql

process_sql("""
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS crsp.tfz_ft;
CREATE TABLE crsp.tfz_ft AS
SELECT
    kytreasnox, tidxfam, ttermtype, ttermlbl, caldt, rdtreasno, rdcrspid
FROM crsp.tfz_idx
INNER JOIN crsp.tfz_dly_ft
USING (kytreasnox);
""")
```

## Common options

- `force=True` refreshes even when the destination already looks current.
- `keep=` and `drop=` trim source columns.
- `obs=` limits rows for testing.
- `alt_table_name=` writes to a different destination table name.
- `create_roles=True` ensures schema roles and grants are created.
- `wrds_schema=` lets the WRDS source schema differ from the destination schema.
- `use_sas=True` uses SAS metadata for freshness checks and destination comments.

## Operational helpers

- `wrds_get_tables()` lists WRDS tables in a schema.
- `process_sql()` executes SQL against the destination PostgreSQL database.
- `set_table_comment()` manages destination table comments directly.

## Related reference pages

- [wrds_update_pg](reference/wrds_update_pg.qmd)
- [wrds_pg_to_pg](reference/wrds_pg_to_pg.qmd)
- [db_schema_tables](reference/db_schema_tables.qmd)
- [wrds_get_tables](reference/wrds_get_tables.qmd)
- [process_sql](reference/process_sql.qmd)
- [set_table_comment](reference/set_table_comment.qmd)
